data.title: Williams-Sonoma beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; raises 2025 operating margin outlook
data.description:
• Williams-Sonoma press release (WSM): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.96 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $1.88B (+4.4% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Q3 comparable brand revenue +4.0%.
  • Merchandise inventories +9.6% to the third quarter LY to $1.5 billion, including incremental tariff costs and a strategic pull forward of receipts.
  • Maintained strong liquidity position of $885 million in cash and $316 million in operating cash flow, enabling the company to deliver returns to stockholders of $347 million through $267 million in stock repurchases and $80 million in dividends.
  • New stock repurchase authorization of $1 billion.
  • Raises 2025 operating margin outlook.

  • OUTLOOK

    • We are reiterating our fiscal 2025 net revenue guidance. We expect annual net revenues in the range of +0.5% to +3.5% inclusive of the impact from the 53rd week in fiscal 2024 (vs. estimated growth of 1.67% Y/Y), with comps in the range of +2.0% to +5.0%.
    • We are raising our guidance on operating margin for fiscal 2025. We now expect an operating margin between 17.8% to 18.1% (with the 53rd week contributing 20bps in fiscal 2024).
    • Our updated guidance includes (i) the new Section 232 tariffs on furniture, (ii) the revised additional tariffs on China of 20%, (iii) the tariffs on India of 50%, (iv) the tariffs on Vietnam of 20%, (v) average tariffs on the rest of the world of 18%, (vi) the steel and aluminum tariff of 50% and (vii) the copper tariff of 50%.
    • For fiscal 2025, we expect annual interest income to be approximately $35 million and our effective tax rate to be approximately 26.0%.
    • Fiscal 2025 is a 52-week year. Our financial statements will be prepared on a 52-week basis in fiscal 2025 versus 53-week basis in fiscal 2024. However, we will report comps on a 52-week versus 52-week comparable basis. All other year-over-year comparisons will be 52-weeks in fiscal 2025 versus 53-weeks in fiscal 2024.
    • Over the long term, we continue to expect mid-to-high single-digit annual net revenue growth with an operating margin in the mid-to-high teens.

data.title: Regeneron gets EU nod for expanded approval of Libtayo
data.description:
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) has received approval from the European Commission for Libtayo (cemiplimab), to be used as an adjuvant treatment for adults with cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma ((CSCC)).

This new approval broadens the existing indication for Libtayo in the European Union, now including patients with advanced CSCC who face a significant risk of their disease returning.

The decision to approve Libtayo is backed by findings from the global Phase 3 C-POST trial, which compared the effectiveness of adjuvant Libtayo against a placebo in patients with CSCC who are at a high risk of recurrence following their surgical and radiation treatments.

data.title: Madison Pacific Properties Inc. reports results
data.description:
• Madison Pacific Properties Inc. press release (MPC:CA): 9M the Company is reporting a net income of $20.9 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $13.6 million); 

  • non-cash operating balances of $8.0 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $9.8 million); and income per share of $0.27 (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $0.22). Net income includes a net gain on the fair value adjustment on investment properties of approximately $18.6 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $7.8 million), losses on fair value adjustment on interest rate swaps of $1.1 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $2.7 million), interest expense of $11.7 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $9.5 million), interest income of $0.6 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: $1.7 million), and equity losses of associate and joint ventures of $1.2 million (nine months ended August 31, 2024: earnings of $0.8 million).

    As at September 30, 2025, the Company owns approximately $758 million in investment properties (December 31, 2024: $724 million).

data.title: Nasdaq and SGX forge a pathway for seamless U.S.–Singapore dual listings
data.description:
Nasdaq (NDAQ) and the Singapore Exchange have unveiled a new partnership aimed at streamlining dual listings between Singapore and the U.S., marking a significant step toward closer integration of two major global financial centers.

The initiative is designed to give companies with market capitalizations of at least $2 billion a more efficient route to tap investors and liquidity across both markets.

Under the proposed framework, regulators are working to create a unified set of disclosure standards that would allow eligible firms to rely on a single offering document for listings in both jurisdictions. This alignment is expected to reduce administrative burdens, shorten timelines, and enhance transparency for issuers and investors alike. 

Market participants say the collaboration could reshape how high-growth companies—from emerging technology innovators to more established corporations—approach international expansion. By offering synchronized access to deep U.S. capital pools and Asia’s fast-growing investor base, the exchanges aim to make cross-border listings more attractive.

Industry analysts note that the move arrives at a time when global fundraising conditions remain competitive, heightening the appeal of simplified regulatory pathways. 

“The whole-of-ecosystem support has been a key driver in turning this vision into reality. For issuers, the proposition is clear: access to U.S. market depth and Asian growth in a streamlined pathway,” Loh Boon Chye, Chief Executive Officer, SGX Group, said.

Market Tracking ETF: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: GitLab in focus as Truist downgrades on 'heightened execution risk'
data.description:
GitLab (GLTB) was in focus on Wednesday as investment firm Truist downgraded the DevOps company, citing “heightened execution risk.”

Shares fell 1.7% in premarket trading.

“We are downgrading GitLab to Hold from Buy as we see the company facing low visibility and heightened execution risk in the face of a business model transition that is being forced by secular changes in the developer tools landscape,” analyst Miller Jump wrote in a note to clients. “At its core, GitLab has been built on a seat-based pricing model, but the widespread adoption of AI coding assistants is beginning to structurally disrupt this approach. Unlike traditional users, AI agents cannot be sold a seat in GitLab’s current model, which undermines the legacy backbone of their revenue. In response, management has indicated plans to incorporate a consumption-based component into the pricing model, beginning in earnest with the general availability release of the Duo Agent platform in late 2025/early 2026.”

Jump also put a $44 price target on GitLab, which competes with Microsoft's (MSFT) GitHub.

Delving deeper, Jump said that management may be too focused on seat expansion, which he sees as “ingrained attachment to a legacy model rather than a reason for optimism about the continuation of the seat-based model going forward.”

“Crucially, while management has communicated that this will be incremental rather than an outright replacement of the per-seat model, the road ahead remains uncertain,” Jump continued. “The reality is that such a transition is being driven by competitive necessity, rather than from a position of strategic strength or innovation leadership. In our experience covering software companies, transitions to recurring/consumption models under these circumstances tend to be fraught, with unpredictable growth trajectories and meaningful risk of operational missteps.”

data.title: Big tech CEOs set to take part in US-Saudi AI investment forum in DC: report
data.description:
CEOs from an array of big tech companies, including Tesla (TSLA), Nvidia (NVDA), Palantir (PLTR), Qualcomm (QCOM), Cisco (CSCO), IBM (IBM), Google (GOOG)(GOOGL), Salesforce (CRM), Adobe (ADBE) and Supermicro (SMCI), will participate in a discussion on artificial intelligence at a U.S.-Saudi Arabia investment forum on Wednesday in Washington, D.C., according to Reuters.

U.S. President Donald Trump is also expected to speak at the event, which will be attended by Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman in what will be his first U.S. trip since 2018, the report said. 

CEOs from energy, defense, health and finance will also take part, including Chevron (CVX), Aramco (ARMCO), Halliburton (HAL), Lockheed Martin (LMT), Boeing (BA), Blackstone (BX) and Pfizer (PFE).

"This conversation will explore the emerging forces shaping the next wave of technological progress, highlighting the architectures, models, and investments powering a more intelligent and interconnected future," the report said, citing a document on the event.

data.title: Nanox to acquire VasoHealthcare IT to accelerate U.S. AI imaging deployment
data.description:
• Nanox (NNOX) agreed to acquire VasoHealthcare IT (VHC IT) from Vaso Corporation for up to $800,000, pending conditions.
  • The deal includes $200,000 cash at closing and up to $600,000 in earnouts tied to two-year revenue retention targets.
  • VHC IT provides nationwide healthcare IT integration, workflow optimization, data migration, training, and go-live support.
  • Nanox plans to integrate VHC IT’s operations with its FDA-cleared Nanox.AI imaging solutions to speed deployment across U.S. healthcare systems.
  • The transaction is expected to be executed and completed within a few weeks.

data.title: Moderna completes end-to-end mRNA manufacturing network in U.S.
data.description:
Moderna (MRNA) announced on Wednesday that it has completed its domestic end-to-end mRNA manufacturing network with the onshoring of drug product manufacturing to its Moderna Technology Center in Norwood, Massachusetts.

The COVID-19 vaccine maker said it has invested more than $140M for the construction, creating hundreds of highly skilled jobs in the biomanufacturing sector.

The biotech identified the development as a significant milestone in its efforts to boost its U.S. manufacturing network amid the Trump administration’s threats to impose tariffs on pharmaceuticals.

Major drugmakers such as Pfizer (PFE) and AstraZeneca (AZN) have reached pricing deals with the U.S. and pledged new domestic investments in exchange for a waiver on tariffs.

“As an American company committed to building and producing in America, we are proud to strengthen our domestic footprint while bringing meaningful new jobs to the community,” CEO Stéphane Bancel remarked.

data.title: DraftKings and FanDuel split from the casino lobby amid prediction markets disruption
data.description:
DraftKings (DKNG) and Flutter Entertainment's (FLUT) FanDuel both resigned from the American Gaming Association this month amid a disagreement about how the U.S. gaming industry should approach prediction markets. 

The American Gaming Association, which represents casino operators such as MGM Resorts International (MGM), Caesars Entertainment, and Wynn Resorts, maintains that prediction markets operate outside traditional state regulatory frameworks and sidestep responsible gaming and consumer protection rules. The AGA supports state-level oversight of prediction markets instead of federal oversight through the CFTC.

However, the two sports betting giants have gone their own way. FanDuel, in partnership with CME Group, is launching its FanDuel Predicts prediction market platform in December. FanDuel, in partnership with CME Group, is launching its “FanDuel Predicts” prediction market platform in December. The app will initially be available in states where traditional online sports betting is still prohibited, letting users wager on various sports outcomes and CME-listed benchmarks. Meanwhile, DraftKings (DKNG) has plans for a DraftKings Predictions platform, targeting launch by year-end 2025 or early 2026, pending regulatory approval. The platform will debut in states without a DraftKings Sportsbook location.

Weighing in on the development, Needham analyst Bernie McTernan thinks prediction markets could drive online sports betting legislation to new states over the medium term and stand as a warning to states looking to increase tax rates on the industry. 

The U.S. states that do not have legalized sports betting are Alabama, Alaska, California, Georgia, Hawaii, Idaho, Minnesota, Oklahoma, South Carolina, Texas, and Utah. Those states all prohibit sports wagering, with legislative efforts either stalled, blocked, or yet to materialize in their respective legislatures. Florida, Minnesota, and Wisconsin only have limited legalized sports betting.

Looking ahead, McTernan pointed to DraftKings' (DKNG) major multi-year partnership with ESPN (DIS) to be the exclusive official sportsbook and odds provider for the media giant to be a general positive and a helpful factor in the launch of a prediction market product due to ESPN's nationwide scale.

Shares of DraftKings (DKNG) are down 14.3% over the last six weeks, while Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) is off 23%.
